threat_model: Temporal retention receipts guard lifecycle decisions that can lead to deletion, anonymization, archive, or review. The risk is a due timestamp being misread as sufficient authority for an irreversible lifecycle action. The mitigation is an approval-certificate and backup-guard boundary inside the same retention receipt, with deterministic blocker codes when due destructive or high-risk action requests omit approval refs, approval certificate hash, or backup guard hash.

required_checks.check: policy bypass check proving destructive and high-risk retention actions declare approval as required when retention checks are active
receipt integrity check proving due deletion is blocked without retention approval refs, approval certificate hash, and backup guard hash
IDOR tenant-scope check proving tenant and legal-hold checks continue to block lifecycle action independent of approval evidence
audit visibility check proving approval evidence fields and approval metadata remain visible in the audited receipt
memory admission check proving retention-active records remain deferred rather than admitted for lifecycle action

mitigations: require retained approval certificate refs before due destructive or high-risk retention action allowance
require retained approval certificate hash before due destructive or high-risk retention action allowance
require backup guard hash when policy requires it
preserve legal hold, tenant scope, source receipt, and timing checks as independent blockers
keep the change local to receipt evaluation with no live deletion executor or provider authority
